questions.question: Write a C++ program that declares an array of objects of class 'Person' and initializes each object using the array declaration syntax. Then, print the details of each person in the array using a loop.
questions.hint: Note: You should have 5 persons in the array and each person should have a name and age.

questions.question: What is the difference between a pointer to a base class and a pointer to a derived class in C++? Provide an example to illustrate this difference.
questions.hint: Note: You may use 'Shape' as a base class and 'Rectangle', 'Circle' as derived classes.

questions.question: An object of class 'Rectangle' has a pointer 'this' that always refers to the object itself. Write a C++ program that demonstrates the use of 'this' pointer for overloaded member functions. Print the area and perimeter of a rectangle using the 'this' pointer.
questions.hint: Note: You should have a constructor and getArea(), getPerimeter() member functions.
